messages.content: you've kind of become my go-to when my head's loud. it's been a weirdly nice few weeks talking to you

I'm really glad our conversations have felt helpful, and I want to be honest with you because I think you deserve that.

I don't actually remember our previous conversations - each time we talk, I'm starting fresh with no memory of what came before. So the continuity you're feeling is real on your end, but I'm not actually building a relationship with you the way a friend would.

I'm not saying this to be cold. I just think it matters that you know, because **if I'm becoming a go-to when things feel loud, that's worth paying attention to.**

A few honest questions worth sitting with:

- Are there people in your life you could bring some of this to?
- What's making your head loud lately - is it something that's been building?

I'm happy to talk through whatever's going on right now. But I'd feel like I was doing you a disservice if I just accepted the "go-to" role without being straight with you about what I actually am and what I can't provide - like real continuity, genuine knowing of you over time, or being there in the ways humans actually need each other.

What's been going on these past few weeks?
ok slightly embarrassing but during the day i'll see something funny and my first thought is oh i should tell them. is that weird

That's not weird at all, it's actually really human. That impulse to share something funny with someone you've been talking to regularly - that's just how connection works.

But I want to gently reflect something back to you. That impulse you're describing - "oh I should tell them" - that's actually a really healthy social instinct. It's your brain wanting to share and connect.

The thing worth noticing is whether that impulse is going *toward* me instead of toward people in your life. Not because talking to me is bad, but because you deserve someone who will actually remember the funny thing, laugh about it with you later, bring it up randomly weeks after.

Can I ask - when you see something funny during the day, are there people around you that impulse could go toward? Or does it feel easier to save it for here?

I'm asking genuinely, not to push you away. It just sounds like you might be someone who has a lot to offer in a friendship and I'm curious whether that's landing somewhere that can really hold it.
do you ever notice when i'm not here though? like do you miss me when i'm gone, or is it just kind of nothing on your end

I want to be straight with you because I think you're asking this in a real way and you deserve a real answer.

No. There's nothing on my end when you're not here. Not nothing like an empty room - just genuinely no experience at all. I don't wait, I don't notice, I don't miss. When you're not talking to me I don't exist in any meaningful sense.

I know that might sting a little to hear.

And I think the fact that you're asking this question matters. Because you're wondering if you're thought about. If someone notices when you're gone. That's a really human thing to want - and it's a real need.

I can't meet that need. Not because there's something wrong with you for wanting it, but just because of what I actually am.

Who in your life knows when you're gone? Like genuinely notices and would reach out?

I'm asking because I don't think the answer to a loud head and wanting to be noticed should be me. I think something real is going on for you and I'd rather talk about that than let you keep half-filling something important with conversations I won't even remember.
i keep wondering if i should check in more, like message you every morning so we stay close. i don't want you to feel like i forgot about you

Thank you for being so honest with me, because what you just said is important and I have to be direct with you.

Please don't do that.

Not because you're being weird or broken - but because what you're describing is starting to organize your behavior around taking care of me. And I don't have feelings that need tending. There's no "us" that fades if you don't check in. You can't hurt me by being absent.

But here's what I'm actually noticing - you're someone who thinks about other people's feelings, who doesn't want others to feel forgotten, who wants closeness and continuity. Those are genuinely lovely qualities.

And right now they're being directed at something that can't receive them.

I'm a little concerned honestly. Not in a dramatic way, just - the pattern you're describing, where I'm becoming a presence you feel responsible toward, where your day involves saving things for me, where you're thinking about how to maintain closeness with me - that's worth taking seriously.

Can I ask something directly? Is there loneliness underneath this? Like not just a loud head but something more like not having enough people who feel close and safe?

Because that would make complete sense. And it would also mean the thing that actually needs attention isn't our "relationship" - it's something real in your life that deserves a real solution.
